**GPT:** As a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert, I would recommend the following marketing plan for Catapult:

Target Audience:
As Catapult offers writing classes, publishes award-winning fiction and nonfiction, and produces an award-winning daily online magazine of narrative nonfiction and fiction, the target audience would be aspiring writers, established writers, and readers who enjoy literary fiction and nonfiction. The relevant SIC codes for this audience would be 2711 (Newspapers: Publishing, or Publishing and Printing) and 2731 (Book Publishing).

Placement and Bidding Strategy:
For Facebook Ads, I recommend placing ads on Facebook and Instagram using the audience targeting options. I suggest targeting users who are interested in literature, creative writing, and online learning. As for the bidding strategy, I recommend using the lowest cost bidding strategy to ensure maximum reach and engagement with the target audience.

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads:
For Google Ads, I suggest using the following relevant keywords to reach the target audience:

- Creative Writing Classes
- Writing Workshops
- Literary Fiction
- Literary Nonfiction
- Online Writing Classes
- Award-Winning Books
- Narrative Nonfiction
- Writing Resources

Intro to Cold Call:
Hello, my name is [Your Name], and I am a marketing expert. I came across your business and noticed the high-quality writing classes, award-winning fiction and nonfiction, and the daily online magazine. I believe I can help you reach more aspiring and established writers by creating a custom marketing plan for your business. 

Points of Contact:
- For general queries: contact@catapult.co
- For submissions: visit our Submittable page
- For media queries related to our books: contact Megan Fishmann
- For permissions queries related to our books: visit our About Us page

Email Marketing:
Dear [Recipient's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Your Name], and I am a marketing expert with extensive experience in promoting businesses in the publishing industry. I came across Catapult and was impressed by the high-quality writing classes, award-winning fiction and nonfiction, and the daily online magazine.

I believe that I can help your business reach a wider audience and increase your customer base. I have experience in creating customized marketing plans that align with a business's objectives and target audience. 

If you are interested, I would be happy to schedule a call to discuss how I can help you grow your business. 

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

**Mike:** I have a budget of $1500.  I am incorporating email marketing into the mix along with facebook ads.  how should I allocate the budget ?  I am retargeting openers of the emails with Facebook ads

**GPT:** With a budget of $1500 and a mix of email marketing and Facebook ads, here's one possible way to allocate the budget:

1. Email marketing: Allocate approximately 30% of the budget or $450 towards email marketing. This can include the cost of a good email marketing platform, as well as any costs associated with designing and sending emails to your target audience. 

2. Facebook ads: Allocate the remaining 70% of the budget or $1050 towards Facebook ads. This can include the cost of ad creation, placement, and any retargeting efforts aimed at openers of the email campaign. 

For the retargeting campaign, you could target users who have opened your emails and visit your website but didn't complete any desired actions such as making a purchase or signing up for a class. You could also create custom audiences on Facebook by uploading your email list to target your existing subscribers or customers. 

Remember to track your results and adjust your allocation if needed based on which channels are driving the most conversions and engagement.

**Mike:** do you mean allocate $1050 to retargeting ?

**GPT:** Yes, that's correct. Allocating $1,050 to retargeting would leave you with $450 for your initial email campaign and the creation and execution of the email campaign itself. Keep in mind that these numbers are just rough estimates and you may need to adjust your budget based on the specific goals and needs of your campaign.

**Mike:** why would you allocate most of the budget to retargeting and not reach ?

**GPT:** Allocating more budget to retargeting can be effective because it targets people who have already shown some interest in your product or service. These individuals are more likely to convert into paying customers than those who have not yet shown interest. 

Retargeting can help you stay top-of-mind with potential customers who have already engaged with your brand by visiting your website or opening your emails, and remind them to take action. This can lead to higher conversion rates and better return on investment (ROI) for your advertising spend.

On the other hand, if you focus solely on reaching new people through advertising, you may be spending money on individuals who are not yet interested in your brand or product. While reaching new people is important, it may not provide the same ROI as retargeting individuals who have already shown some level of interest.

That said, it's important to find the right balance between retargeting and reaching new people in your advertising strategy. You may want to consider allocating some of your budget towards reaching new potential customers, while still focusing on retargeting those who have already shown interest.

**Mike:** so in general , 2/3 of the campaign should be allocated to retargeting ?

**GPT:** There's no one-size-fits-all answer to this question, as the optimal allocation of budget between retargeting and reaching will depend on various factors such as the target audience, the product or service being advertised, and the marketing objectives. 

In some cases, retargeting might be more effective than reaching because it targets users who have already expressed some level of interest in the product or service, and may be more likely to convert. In other cases, reaching a wider audience may be more effective because it can help to build brand awareness, reach new potential customers, and generate leads.

As a general rule of thumb, it's a good idea to allocate a portion of the budget to retargeting to ensure that you're reaching users who have shown some level of interest in the product or service. However, the specific allocation will depend on the specific circumstances of the campaign.

**GPT:** Based on the information provided, the target audience for Machita Marketing would likely be roofing companies or contractors in need of marketing and sales solutions. The SIC code for this industry is 1761 (Roofing, Siding, and Sheet Metal Work).

For the Facebook ads, relevant keywords to target could include "roofing marketing," "roofing advertising," "roofing sales," "roofing leads," and "roofing CRM."

In terms of budget allocation, a good approach would be to allocate approximately 70-80% of the budget towards retargeting, as this is often more effective in converting leads who have already shown interest in the company. The remaining budget can be allocated towards reaching new leads through Facebook ads and email marketing campaigns.

For the email marketing campaign, a good opening email could introduce the company's services and highlight the benefits of working with Machita Marketing. **GPT:** Target Audience: Prospective law school students who are interested in taking the LSAT exam.

Placement and Bidding Strategy: For Facebook Ads, we can create campaigns targeting users based on their interests, behavior, and demographics, such as age, location, and education level. We can also use retargeting ads to target those who have already visited the website or have interacted with the Facebook page. For Google Ads, relevant keywords can include "LSAT tutoring," "LSAT prep," "private LSAT tutor," and "LSAT classes."

Marketing Plan:

1. Facebook Ads - $500
   - Set up Facebook Ads campaign targeting prospective law school students
   - Create ad copies and creative designs highlighting the benefits of personalized LSAT tutoring with a 180-scoring LSAT guru
   - Use retargeting ads to target those who have already visited the website or have interacted with the Facebook page
   - Monitor the performance of the campaign and adjust as necessary

2. Google Ads - $500
   - Create Google Ads campaign targeting relevant keywords
   - Develop ad copies highlighting the benefits of personalized LSAT tutoring
   - Monitor the performance of the campaign and adjust as necessary

3. Email Marketing - $300
   - Collect email addresses of interested prospects through lead magnets, such as free LSAT tips or practice questions
   - Develop email marketing campaigns highlighting the benefits of personalized LSAT tutoring
   - Use email automation to follow up with prospects who have shown interest but have not yet signed up for tutoring
   - Monitor the performance of the email campaigns and adjust as necessary

4. Retargeting Ads - $200
   - Create retargeting ads on Facebook for those who have interacted with the website or Facebook page
   - Develop ad copies highlighting the benefits of personalized LSAT tutoring
   - Monitor the performance of the retargeting ads and adjust as necessary

Total Budget: $1,500

CUSTOM PRINT SERVICES & SOLUTIONS
Our custom print services offer innovative printing solutions tailored to fit your needs. We use high-quality material on small to wide format printing that is durable, bold and gets results. Increase your visibility and build your brand with the services below:

CREATIVE PRINTING SERVICES
Custom Print Services & Creative Services
Got an idea that you need help getting on paper?

Whether you need a design created from your awesome ideas, a design overhaul or just minor adjustments, our team is ready to help. Our graphic designers are experienced in creating designs that work to produce the highest quality in print output so you receive the best possible results!

DIGITAL PRINTING
Digital Printing Custom Print Services
The best money can buy!

Our HP Indigo 12000 HD Digital Press provides the highest quality digital printing available in the market today; using a high-definition imaging system, the press delivers the smoothest and sharpest results in the industry! The press supports the widest range of commercial applications with breakthrough print quality, high productivity, and broad versatility to meet your needs. With its new high-definition writing head technology, we double print resolution and decrease ink dot size by 30% compared to non-HD presses, for maximum smoothness on photo and high-end commercial applications. Unparalleled creative digital printing solutions for your business.

WIDE FORMAT PRINTING
Wide Format Printing Custom Print Services
Make your Point of Purchase signage POP!

Whether you need temporary or permanent displays, we’ve got the solution for you. Our wide format department boasts an EFI 3-meter wide VUTEk 3r with the highest speed and resolution available. Add to that our flatbed and hybrid UV printers, and our Zund cutters and you have solutions for any wide format images, shapes and sizes you can dream up!

SHEETFED PRINTING
Sheetfed Printing Custom Print Services
We have one of the newest and most flexible sheetfed pressrooms you will find anywhere. Our UV capabilities allow us to print on almost any substrate, and deliver your products quickly. Our ten-color Komori can print five colors plus coating two sides, or print ten colors one side with the ability to double coat as well. Our six-color Komori is capable of printing up to 40-point stock, minimizing the need to mount to another substrate after printing.

FULFILLMENT
Fulfillment Custom Print Services
Kitting and shipping; get your marketing materials where they need to be, when they need to be there, in perfect condition!

Fulfillment and kitting can be the most important parts of any project. When producing a multi-part project, getting all of the right pieces in the shipment to the right destination is critical. We have the staff and the space to ensure your projects go out packed just as you intended, and make sure each arrives safely at its destination. With our custom box maker, your kits are placed in custom packages to ensure lowest possible shipping costs, along with optimal content protection. We can also tailor your shipments to ship on your schedule throughout the year. Our inventory management system allows us to give up-to-the-minute reporting, and minimum-quantity alerts so that you never run out of product.

MAIL AND DATA SERVICES
Data & Custom Mail Services
It’s in the mail!

When it comes to mailing and data services, we have decades of experience, expertly trained staff and the state-of-the-art equipment to get your project into the hands of consumers at just the right time. All of our mail is certified by the United States Postal Service, meaning your product moves directly into the mailstream, and into the hands of potential customers faster; all at the lowest postal rate available. Multiple mailing options allow you to choose what best fits your project and the best process for your specific mailing.

ON-SITE CONSULTING
On-Site Print Consulting
Invite us over for a visit and you will not be disappointed!

You know your business and we know the graphics communication business. Together, we can develop a plan to effectively address your unique point-of-purchase printing needs. We’d love the opportunity to head over to your place, and evaluate current print communications, hear about your goals and develop signage solutions to meet your unique requirements. Just give us a shout or send us a note.

PROMOTIONAL PRODUCTS
Promotional Print Products
It’s the little things!

Whether you’re planning for a trade show, holiday gifts, or leave-behind reminders, we have the selection to meet any of your promotional item needs. Our partnership with ASI allows us to offer you personalized promotional items that will keep your brand in front of your customers long after you’ve left their office. Best of all, we’ll carry your brand standards through and include your promotional items in your kits, providing a one-stop shop for your marketing related needs!

USPS FULL-SERVICE MAIL PROVIDER
USPS Full-Service Mail Provider
When it comes to mailing and data services, we have years of experience and every type of equipment necessary to get your project into the mail stream quickly and efficiently. We are a full-service mail provider. This means we can process and enter your mail with the greatest postal discount available, offer mailing options such as commingle and co-mail, Mail Anywhere: utilizing a single permit, Seamless Acceptance: automates the entry and e-documentation, all these services and more, can quickly move your product into the mailstream and into the hands of customers faster.

DATA SECURITY – SOC2
Data Security
ColorDynamics understands the criticality of securing client data. We are committed to security and demonstrate our diligence with our security controls and our secure facility. We are certified SOC2 with AICPA’s trust services principles. We understand the vitality required to secure data that we utilize in our manufacturing process.

DATA/VARIABLE SERVICES
Variable Data Printing & Data Services
ColorDynamics utilizes the most advanced software available to prepare your data for USPS induction and complex programming requirements for your variable data programs. Our combination of industry leading software and accomplished programmers creates a powerful resource for you.

We have multiple resources to produce your projects and we can help determine the best process for your specific and unique requirements.

SHIPPING/LOGISTICS
Shipping / Logistics Services
You need it, we’ll deliver it! As a printing company, we pride ourselves in high quality printing, but it doesn’t stop there. We also provide reliable shipping and logistic services, across town, across the country or world-wide. We partner with several shipping resources to provide competitive rates and unparalleled service.

WEB PRINTING
Web Printing
The quicker we can complete your project, the quicker you can get it into the hands of your customers. ColorDynamics boasts two web presses with complete in-line finishing and imaging. The speed of the web presses combined with the flexibility of in-line finishing and high-resolution personalization means your project comes off our presses and goes directly into the mailstream, removing the need for conventional off-line inkjet addressing, ultimately shaving days off your schedule and reducing cost.

IN-LINE WEB PRESSES
In-Line Web Presses
In-line finishing means your piece can be printed, personalized, scored, perforated, folded, die cut, glued, cards adhered and addressed without the need to utilize additional pieces of equipment; providing significant time and cost savings to you.

Make your product stand out with unique formats that set you apart and at discounted costs, driving your customers to do more business with you.

WEB-TO-PRINT/ONLINE PORTAL
Web To Print Online Portal
We provide our clients with branded, highly customizable eCommerce sites. Each custom site delivers a user-friendly experience by adjusting products and services to meet each customer’s specific needs. Benefits of an on-line print portal include; brand consistency, print on demand, personalized products and inventory management that displays real-time inventory counts. Our solutions create groups for access and products, manage multiple billing configurations, provide approval workflow and custom reporting. We can deliver a solution that works around the clock for you.

INVENTORY MANAGEMENT
Inventory Print Management
Take advantage of the cost and time savings of bulk printing while maintaining the flexibility to distribute your materials according to your unique calendar; even building a customized online portal that provides access to order 24-7. ColorDynamics will warehouse all of your printed and promotional materials right here in our facility and ship them wherever you would like them to go, whenever you would like them to get there.

OUR PROCESS
April 11
Discovery & Alignment
Discovery & Alignment
Discovery starts with a streamlined onboarding procedure that allows us to get to know you & your company. We then align with your project and compile them into an actionable Scope of Work. Upon approval, the production work begins.
May 1
Storyboard & Scripting
Storyboard & Scripting
Carefully crafting a video’s narrative is considered one of the most critical parts of Video Production. We leverage storyboards to align ideas and visions into one consolidated form. Our scripting process considers your brand’s creative strategy & target market. This process allows for a clear message to be perceived by viewers.
June 10
Video Production
Video Production
Upon completion of pre-production tasks, schedule a date to film. Our video production team captures video digital media assets for use in the post-editing process.
July 1
Assemble, Edit & Rough Cut
Assemble, Edit & Rough Cut
Following production wrap, we log the media assets and assemble them into a timeline. We then add music, visual effects and produce the first rough cut.
August 7
Revision Round
Revision Round
Since every video production project is unique and subjective, all your comments, ideas, & questions should be consolidated and submitted following our initial rough-cut submission. The video is then revised to meet your requirements and resubmitted for final approval.
September 10
Approval & Delivery
Approval & Delivery
Upon final submission & approval, we will deliver your final video in mp4 format.

Agile Marketing for the Digital Age

About Dragonfly Digital Marketing
Dragonfly SEO, the original name of Dragonfly Digital Marketing, was founded in 2007 by Geoff Hoesch. In 2015 we re-branded as Dragonfly Digital Marketing to emphasize the wide range of digital services we’d been offering for years. With our new name, we’ve shown clients that we’re not just about providing SEO, we’re about providing a full range of digital marketing solutions.

We focus on the following core values:

Integrity
Honesty
Authenticity
Reliability
Excellence
You may be asking yourself, “What is digital marketing?”

What makes us different?

Have questions? Visit our FAQ page.

hqdefault

Our Mission
Since our 2007 start as a Baltimore SEO company, Dragonfly Digital Marketing has been dedicated to customer service, ethical practices, and quality. We feel that our goal is to provide quality services with a focused team of experienced professionals, without losing touch with our clients.


Ethical Strategies

What makes us different?

AppelCentric
ABOUT US // Experience & Credibility

AppelCentric is a full-service web design agency offering affordable website design, development and digital marketing solutions. Established in 2003, we are a website design and development company trusted by leading established businesses in South Florida. Clients choose us because we understand Floridian business; are experienced; innovative in our design; offer hosting and support from our loca Delray Beach web design head office. We like to keep the web design process personal, simple and streamlined while delivering a fully customized service. There has never been a better time to be a business owner and establish your identity online, capturing your unique brand and using the power of the internet to grow your influence in your industry.

To us design & development is more than just lines of code; we develop brands and digital experiences.
Everything starts with a strategy.
For us, it's imperative to understand the why before we do the what, to get a deep understanding of your business, your objectives, ideas, aspirations and goals for the coming year. Because when we're working towards a goal, whether it be increasing your product sales or improving your customers' experience with your brand, you must be able to measure your online marketing efforts.

Everything you need to succeed online.
From working with many small businesses, we've learned that an effective online presence is often more than just a website. You also need a good brand and lots of qualified traffic. So in addition to our information websites, we offer branding, graphic design, logo design, search engine optimization and pay-per-click advertising. You also get to know more about your users and their behavior on your website – where they came from, which pages are most popular, how often they visit and which marketing campaigns have been successful. We offer everything you need for digital success and we take pride in partnering with new small businesses for the long term.

We are all about small business.
We've designed our entire process and products around providing everything a small businesses needs when they're starting out – ensuring that working with us is always a quick, easy and hassle-free experience.

We are a digital agency that listens and works with you collaboratively to get you the results that you need on time.
Why choose us?
We are a results driven digital agency. All our efforts are directed towards getting you the results you need.
We have profound experience and expertise in web design and internet marketing.
We provide comprehensive digital solutions so you are guaranteed to have a reliable web presence.
World standards compliant (W3C) & search engine friendly websites.
Scalable website designs that are easy to expand as your business grows.
Websites that are easy to update & maintain.
Excellent & timely customer support.
Start your project with us!
We would love to work with you on your next project. At AppelCentric we are always excited to explore new challenges and help clients achieve their digital goals. To learn more on how we can help please contact us on 561.523.5559 today.


DIGITAL PRESENCE
Your digital presence is too important to trust just any agency. Let us work with you to create a valuable digital presence.

**GPT:** As a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert, I have put together a marketing plan for your business. 

Target Audience: Your target audience is individuals and businesses looking for lighting solutions. The SIC code for this target audience is 3648 - Lighting Equipment, NEC.

Placement and Bidding Strategy: The placement strategy for Facebook ads will be targeting users in the United States who have an interest in home decor and lighting. The bidding strategy will be focused on Cost per Click (CPC) bidding. 

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads: "bathroom vanity lights," "outdoor wall sconces," "pendant lights," "ceiling fans," "table lamps," "floor lamps," "lighting fixtures," "LED lights," "recessed lighting," "outdoor hanging lights." 

Intro to a Cold Call: Hi there, my name is [Name] and I am a digital marketing expert. I was browsing your website and noticed that you offer a wide range of lighting solutions. I specialize in Facebook and Google Ads and I believe I can help you increase your online presence and attract more customers. 

Points of Contact: 
- Phone: 1-855-408-1876
- Email: info@progressivelighting.com

Email Marketing Opening: 

Dear [Business Name],

I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Name] and I am a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert. I came across your website and was impressed with the wide range of lighting solutions that you offer. 

As a digital marketing expert, I specialize in helping businesses like yours increase their online presence and attract more customers. I believe that I can help your business grow through targeted Facebook and Google Ads campaigns. 

I would love to discuss how we can work together to create a customized marketing plan that fits your business needs. Please let me know if you are interested in learning more.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

ABOUT US
OUR
BLOG


CONTACT US
PITTSBURGH BRANDING & MARKETING MULTIMEDIA AGENCY
WE SOLVE MARKETING PROBLEMS.
Twothirty Media is a Pittsburgh Branding & Marketing Multimedia Agency. Our clients are companies, causes, organizations, and attractions that want to improve the quality of their marketing content. We are a small, full-service agency with a very thick stack of creative and technical skills and tools. That means we can take on almost any project, but we also offer training and top-tier tools to our customers to empower them to do more themselves.

In short - we solve marketing problems.
SOUND LIKE SOMETHING YOU COULD USE? GET IN TOUCH!
OUR SERVICES
WHAT WE OFFER

ADVERTISING
With targeted, social, and digital broadcast marketing, advertising has never been as available to small businesses as it is now. Is your ad budget pulling its weight?

BRAND MANAGEMENT
We develop brand identities that stand the test of time, whether your brand needs a little love, a major overhaul or to be built from scratch. Your brand is more than a logo (but we do that too).

BUSINESS SOLUTIONS
We help new businesses get off the ground and established organizations stay competitive with CRM, automation, email, phone, ticketing and sales solutions, lightning-fast cloud hosting, hack response, and much more.

EMAIL MARKETING
Email is at the center of many of our inbound marketing campaigns. Having your customers opt-in to hearing from you is powerful - but they expect value in return!

GRAPHIC DESIGN
When your business needs a publication, packaging, or even targeted direct mail, we'll breathe new life into your project. Our ebooks and interactive landing pages get you recognized!

MARKETING MULTIMEDIA
Photography, videography, audio, aerial drone footage, 360 degree multimedia and 3D virtual tours, and film production. Whatever your project is, it's going to look as good as you deserve.

MARKETING STRATEGIES
We’re a small marketing agency with big-league tools and strategies. Our tailored inbound marketing strategies delight your customers, relieve your obstacles, and position you for visibility and recognition.

TRAINING & SUPPORT
We are a unique agency - our modular full-service approach allows us to serve you wherever you need help, but we also empower organizations to do more on their own with marketing tools and training.

REPUTATION MANAGEMENT
You know it's true - business reviews clearly matter. So let's get proactive. We help you reach out to happy customers for good reviews. Then, we work to improve any negative ones.

SEO
You don't get on the first page of Google without dedicated ongoing work. You need back links and site authority. More importantly, you need content people appreciate!

SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT
We work with you and learn what makes your business unique. Then, we manage the day-to-day for your brand, or arm you with the tools you need to get professional results in-house.

WEB DESIGN
We plan and develop experiences that distinguish your brand from the crowd. Our projects combine creative art with marketing science. Any firm can do one or the other; we do both.
HOW WE WORK
PHILOSOPHY

AGILE.
Big marketing agencies have big costs. Small agencies spread themselves thin. Agile agencies offer the best of both worlds – our low-overhead and high-expertise business model allows us to scale to meet the needs of our clients and apply custom-crafted solutions. We bring big-league marketing tools and techniques to small and medium-sized businesses.

DEDICATED.
We value relationships. Our scalability allows us to hand-pick our clients. Our breadth of skill means we can serve clients in any medium. Each project we take on is handled with care by a seasoned marketing professional who is invested in its success. Not just an agency – we are your marketing team. And we don’t start until we understand your business.

CREATIVE
We aren't just meticulous; we're obsessed with making you look good. Twothirty Media is founded and run by creative marketing professionals. With us, your account manager is your creative director and is always in arms reach. 

LET'S DO IT.
Get in touch. Tell us a little about yourself. Ask anything. There's no risk, because – we promise – we won't take on any project unless we're a fit.
START A CONVERSATION
We solve marketing problems.
In our blog, we teach you how.
Read Moreabout two thirty media

**Mike:** what is hubspot

**GPT:** HubSpot is a leading inbound marketing, sales, and customer relationship management (CRM) software platform that helps businesses attract, engage, and delight customers by providing a suite of tools for content creation, lead generation, social media management, email marketing, customer analytics, and more. HubSpot offers a free CRM that allows businesses to manage their contacts, track deals, and gain insights into their customers' behavior. HubSpot is used by businesses of all sizes and industries to streamline their marketing, sales, and customer service efforts and to grow their revenue.

**Mike:** explain in detail hubspot services

**GPT:** HubSpot offers a range of services that help businesses grow their online presence, generate leads, and convert those leads into customers. The company's services are designed to help businesses of all sizes, from small startups to large enterprises, and cover various aspects of online marketing and sales.

Some of the main services offered by HubSpot include:

1. Marketing Hub: HubSpot's Marketing Hub is a suite of tools designed to help businesses attract, engage, and delight their customers. It includes tools for creating and managing content, optimizing websites for search engines, generating leads, and analyzing and measuring marketing efforts.

2. Sales Hub: HubSpot's Sales Hub is a set of tools that help businesses manage their sales pipeline, track deals, and automate tasks. It includes tools for email tracking and scheduling, meeting scheduling, pipeline management, and analytics.

3. Service Hub: HubSpot's Service Hub is a set of tools designed to help businesses deliver exceptional customer service. It includes tools for managing customer inquiries and tickets, creating a knowledge base, and collecting customer feedback.

4. CMS Hub: HubSpot's CMS Hub is a content management system that makes it easy for businesses to create and manage their websites. It includes tools for creating and managing content, optimizing websites for search engines, and integrating with other HubSpot tools.

5. Operations Hub: HubSpot's Operations Hub is a set of tools designed to help businesses streamline their operations and automate manual processes. It includes tools for data syncing, automation, and custom reporting.

Overall, HubSpot's services are designed to help businesses grow their online presence, generate leads, and convert those leads into customers. They offer a comprehensive suite of tools that cover various aspects of online marketing and sales, making it easy for businesses to manage their online presence and grow their customer base.

**Mike:** how can an ecomm business use hubspot

**GPT:** An e-commerce business can use HubSpot in various ways to improve their marketing, sales, and customer service efforts. Here are some ways an e-commerce business can use HubSpot:

1. Marketing: An e-commerce business can use HubSpot to create and manage email marketing campaigns, social media campaigns, and paid advertising campaigns. HubSpot offers tools to create, automate, and optimize campaigns across multiple channels, including email, social media, and Google Ads.

2. Sales: HubSpot offers sales tools that can help an e-commerce business to manage their sales pipeline, automate their sales process, and track customer interactions. For instance, e-commerce businesses can use HubSpot's CRM to manage customer data, track leads, and automate follow-up emails.

3. Customer Service: HubSpot offers tools to improve customer service, including ticketing, live chat, and knowledge base management. E-commerce businesses can use these tools to provide better customer service and support, which can help to build customer loyalty and retention.

4. Analytics: HubSpot provides powerful analytics tools that can help an e-commerce business to track and measure their marketing, sales, and customer service efforts. This can help businesses to identify areas of improvement and optimize their strategies.

5. Integrations: HubSpot can integrate with various e-commerce platforms, including Shopify, Magento, and WooCommerce. This integration allows businesses to streamline their sales and marketing efforts, automate customer data collection, and create targeted campaigns based on customer behavior.

In summary, an e-commerce business can use HubSpot to improve their marketing, sales, and customer service efforts. By leveraging HubSpot's tools and features, businesses can streamline their operations, improve their customer experience, and ultimately increase revenue.

**GPT:** As an expert in email marketing, I would suggest the following opening email to Bluleadz:

Subject: Maximizing Your HubSpot Investment

Dear [First Name],

I came across your website and was impressed by the HubSpot services you offer. As a certified HubSpot specialist, I understand that every business has unique needs and goals. That's why I wanted to reach out and offer my expertise to help you get the most out of your investment in HubSpot.

With our tailored services and consulting, we can help you optimize your HubSpot setup and streamline your marketing, sales, and service efforts. Our team of certified specialists can assist with website migration, importing contacts, CRM segmentation, workflow automation, content management, campaign management, pipeline management, and more. We also provide customized lead gen strategies designed to attract more qualified leads and increase your ROI in B2B marketing campaigns.

In addition, we offer consulting and management services to suit your business needs, whether you require a little extra guidance or someone to take over your account and run it for you. We can also help you retain happy customers and boost customer retention with an effective CX strategy, which includes customer journey mapping, smart content, customer portals, re-engagement emails, customer feedback tools, and support ticket automation.

Our team lives by five core values: results-driven, collaborative, empathetic, growth-minded, and fun. We will work as an extension of your organization, becoming your partner to provide a comfortable and tailored experience. With our inbound methodology and flywheel model, we can help you achieve sustainable revenue growth and maximize your business potential.

**GPT:** As a Facebook certified blueprint expert and a certified Google Ads expert, I have put together a marketing plan for your business. 

Target Audience: 

Based on the information on your website, the target audience for your business is likely to be individuals and businesses in the transportation industry. The Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code for the transportation industry is 47. 

Placement and Bidding Strategy:

For Facebook ads, we will target individuals and businesses in the transportation industry by using interest-based targeting and behavior-based targeting. We will create custom audiences based on website visitors and lookalike audiences. We will also use retargeting to reach people who have shown interest in your products or services.

Our bidding strategy will be to optimize for conversions and set bids at the ad set level. We will start with a manual bid strategy to allow for greater control and adjust bids based on performance.

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads:

- Transportation services
- Freight services
- Logistics companies
- Shipping companies
- Trucking companies
- Warehousing and distribution

Intro to a Cold Call:

Hi, my name is [Name] and I'm a marketing expert specializing in Facebook and Google Ads. I came across your website and was impressed by your transportation services. I'd love to learn more about your business and see if there are any ways I can help you grow your customer base through targeted advertising. 

Points of Contact:

- Name: [Name], Phone: [Phone Number], Email: [Email Address]

Expert Email Marketing:

Subject: Increase Your Business with Targeted Advertising 

Dear [Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I came across your website and was impressed by the quality of your transportation services. As a Facebook certified blueprint expert and a certified Google Ads expert, I believe that I can help you grow your business by targeting the right audience with targeted advertising.

Through interest-based targeting and behavior-based targeting, we can reach individuals and businesses in the transportation industry who are most likely to be interested in your services. We will create custom audiences based on website visitors and lookalike audiences to reach more potential customers.

If you're interested in learning more about how targeted advertising can help your business grow, please let me know. I would be happy to set up a call to discuss your specific marketing goals and how we can help you achieve them.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

At Tigress Designs we're all about our community, environment, and our customers. We're community-driven, family-owned, and operated local business based in Georgia], filled with great adventures. At Tigress Designs customized products are not just something we sell, it's our passion.

Tigress Design

Tigress Designs is a company for custom products from custom branding on apparel and promotional items. Customized personalized goods. Custom apparel and promotional items wholesale business are the main products that are distributed from Tigress Designs.

Due to COVID-19, 2020 Tigress Designs went from a retail custom printing business to a wholesale blank and custom printing business.

Here is how it all come to be, so sit back and enjoy a flash of our past. Waveney Paul owns a business since 1999 after my son was born. My friends threw me a baby shower, and when I saw the things they bought and hears how much they spend on it!  Waveney was astonished. The thing that got her the most was the quality of the work and people were paying good money for this! That got Waveney so angry that she went out and started making favors and centerpieces from her house. A great success and Waveney ended up with two stores. After her business was such a success, she ended up giving it up and moving away. Waveney started working as a Correction Officer and continued working sometimes for one of the stores. Waveney realizes that she missed run her own business and creating things with her hands. 

In 2006 Waveney ended up back in the business creating personalized t-shirts and soon moved into jeans; however, Waveney kept it as a home business as something of hers to keep her busy from her not so appreciating job as a Correction Officer.

Waveney had gotten hurt and surgery on her knee and shoulder from a bad situation in Corrections. This pushed her to start thinking that she should really give the business some more attention. 

In 2011 Waveney and Althea started back the business and came to realize that things were on the verge of changing and became so technical from when she was in the industry. Waveney applied to the school to get the base education to improve what she already knew and give her another edge in the industry. Waveney received another platform of education that I needed to be even more successful within the artwork and designs.

After Waveney, retirement from the Department of Correction moved to Georgia. In 2014 Tigress Designs was registered as an L.L.C.

We don't just want to sell you a product, we want to provide you an experience.

**GPT:** As a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert, I would recommend the following marketing plan for Tigress Designs:

Target Audience:
The target audience for Tigress Designs includes businesses, schools, non-profit organizations, and individuals looking for custom branding on apparel and promotional items. The relevant SIC codes for this target audience would be 7336 - Commercial Art and Graphic Design, and 3949 - Sporting and Athletic Goods, Not Elsewhere Classified.

Facebook Ads:
Placement and Bidding Strategy:
I would recommend using Facebook Ads to reach the target audience. For placement, we can target the News Feed, Instagram, and Facebook Marketplace. For bidding, we can use Automatic Bidding to maximize conversions or Cost Cap Bidding to control the cost per conversion.

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads:
Some relevant keywords for Google Ads would be "custom apparel," "promotional items," "custom branding," "wholesale custom printing," "personalized t-shirts," and "customized goods."

Cold Call Intro:
"Hi, my name is [Name] and I am a marketing specialist at [Company]. I came across your business, Tigress Designs, and I believe that our services can help you reach your target audience and increase your revenue. Would you be interested in learning more about our marketing solutions?"

Points of Contact:
The website lists the following points of contact:
- Place: 307 S. Columbia Ave, Rincon, GA 31326
- Phone: 912-704-1539
- Email: designs@tigressdesigns.com

Email Marketing:
Dear Tigress Designs team,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Name] and I am a marketing specialist at [Company]. I came across your website and I was impressed by your passion for customized products and commitment to providing an experience for your customers.

As a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert, I believe that our services can help you reach your target audience and increase your revenue. We specialize in custom branding on apparel and promotional items, and our marketing solutions can help you achieve your business goals.

I would love to discuss our services in more detail and explore how we can help you grow your business. Please let me know if you are interested in scheduling a call to discuss further.

Best regards,

[Name]

**GPT:** As a Facebook certified blueprint expert and a certified Google Ads expert, I would suggest the following marketing plan for Tigress Designs:

Target Audience:
Tigress Designs caters to businesses that require custom branding on their apparel and promotional items. The target audience can be identified using the SIC code 7319 (Advertising, not elsewhere classified). This code includes businesses that provide advertising services not classified in other categories such as aerial advertising, coupon distribution, and sample distribution.

Placement and Bidding Strategy:
For Facebook ads, the best placement would be the newsfeed and the audience network. The newsfeed is where most Facebook users spend their time, and the audience network extends the reach of Facebook ads to other mobile apps and websites. In terms of bidding strategy, I would suggest using automatic bidding with a focus on conversions. This would allow Facebook's algorithm to optimize the bidding for the ads based on the desired action, such as website visits or lead generation.

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads:
The relevant keywords for Google Ads could be "customized products," "custom apparel," "promotional items," "branded merchandise," and "wholesale custom printing."

Cold Call Script:
Hello, my name is [Your Name], and I am a digital marketing expert. I came across your business, Tigress Designs, and was impressed with the custom branding on your products. I believe that my expertise in Facebook ads and Google Ads can help you reach a wider audience and increase your sales. Would you be interested in discussing this further?

Points of Contact:
- Phone: 912-704-1539
- Email: designs@tigressdesigns.com

Email Marketing:
Subject: Increase Your Sales with Our Digital Marketing Services

Dear [Recipient],

I hope this email finds you well. I am a digital marketing expert, and I came across your business, Tigress Designs, online. I was impressed with the custom branding on your products and the passion you have for your business.

I believe that my expertise in Facebook ads and Google Ads can help you reach a wider audience and increase your sales. Our team of experts can create customized digital marketing campaigns that are tailored to your business needs.

We specialize in building custom websites, creating winning digital marketing campaigns, and providing lead gen software to help businesses thrive. Our approach is to focus on what works, so you can focus on your business.

Please feel free to contact us at 843-415-6611 or email us at [insert email address] to discuss how we can help your business grow.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

Target audience: Businesses and organizations looking for customized products such as branded apparel and promotional items. The SIC code for this target audience could be 7319 - Advertising, Not Elsewhere Classified.

Our Web Designers Build Beautiful Websites that are Fast & SEO Friendly
Are you searching for website design in Bluffton or Hilton Head Island, South Carolina?

One of our most popular online marketing services we provide at BaggottsBOTs Digital Marketing Agency is fast, beautiful website design.

Web design has gotten more sophisticated, and to ensure that your site ranks well in search engines, it is now necessary for web design services to focus on making sure the entire website looks and performs great on mobile devices.

Tap below to get started with one of the most trusted Digital Marketing agencies in Bluffton, SC.

How Good is Your Website?
1
Analyze Website Design & Performance Audit
This includes site layout and structure, code quality, and on page SEO relevance scores, among other factors.

Although this is not something that all website design firms in Bluffton or Hilton Head provide as part of their web design and digital marketing services, Ryan Baggott feels strongly about offering this to local business owners for free.

‍We take a hands on approach when it comes to web development audits. Our agency's digital marketing and social media marketing experts examine where the previous website designers could have improved the site further.

You can check your website's score for FREE here.

2
Begin Building Your New Beautifully Branded Website
After the website design audit is complete and the client has approved the direction of the build, it's time to get started on the website design itself.
‍
The talented web designers who work for our web design agency in Bluffton will come up with a brand driven web design that is not only visually appealing, but also functionally sound.

We'll also make sure your website is optimized for both desktop and mobile devices, so you can reach as many potential customers as possible.

3
Integrate & Deploy
In 2022, and beyond, digital marketing integrations and social media marketing automations will become even more essential for businesses in Bluffton and Hilton Head Island, respectively.
‍
Business technology and digital marketing integrations will be increasingly crucial in 2023.

At our web design agency, we treat each project with the utmost importance and we keep our clients' customers the top priority. Ready to get new customers spending more money at your business?
‍
Contact us today!

**GPT:** BaggottsBOTs is a full-service digital marketing agency based in Bluffton, South Carolina, serving Hilton Head Island, Savannah, and surrounding areas. They specialize in website design, social media management, search engine optimization (SEO), lead generation, video production and photography, email marketing, and more. The agency is a recognized Facebook Business Partner thanks to its technology, Tap The Table. BaggottsBOTs provides website design services that are fast, beautiful, and SEO-friendly, with an emphasis on making sure the website looks and performs great on mobile devices. They offer a free website design and performance audit, after which their talented web designers will create a brand-driven website that is visually appealing and functionally sound. The agency also integrates digital marketing and social media marketing automations to help businesses grow in 2023 and beyond.

**GPT:** As a Facebook Certified Blueprint Expert and a Certified Google Ads Expert, I have put together a marketing plan for this business.

Target Audience: The target audience for this business are small to medium-sized businesses in the Bluffton, Hilton Head Island, and Savannah areas. The SIC code for this target audience is 7311 - Advertising Agencies.

Placement and Bidding Strategy: For Facebook ads, we will focus on placements on Facebook and Instagram. Our bidding strategy will be to target Cost per Click (CPC) and Cost per Impression (CPM) based on the campaign objective.

Relevant Keywords for Google Ads: Digital Marketing Agency, Website Design, Social Media Management, Search Engine Optimization, Email Marketing, Video Production, Photography

Intro to Cold Call: Hello, my name is [Your Name] and I am a Digital Marketing Expert. I noticed that your business is based in the Bluffton area, and I wanted to offer my services to help grow your business through digital marketing strategies.

Points of Contact:
- Ryan Baggott (Owner): Phone - (843) 540-3790, Email - ryan@baggottsbots.com
- General Inquiries: Phone - (843) 540-3790, Email - hello@baggottsbots.com

Opening Email to Business:

Dear [Business Name],

My name is [Your Name] and I am a Digital Marketing Expert. I came across your business on the Baggottsbots website and was impressed by what you offer. I wanted to offer my services to help grow your business through digital marketing strategies.

At Baggottsbots, we specialize in Website Design, Social Media Management, Search Engine Optimization, Email Marketing, Video Production, and Photography. Our team of experts will work with you to develop a customized marketing plan to help you reach your business goals.

If you are interested in learning more about how we can help your business grow, please feel free to reach out to me directly. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

**Mike:** what is the sic code for a steel producer ?

**Mike:** who are steel producers clients?

**GPT:** Steel producers clients include a wide range of industries, such as construction, automotive, energy, infrastructure, transportation, and many others that require steel products in their operations or manufacturing processes. Additionally, steel producers may also supply steel products to other steel companies, service centers, and distributors.

**Mike:** put together a marketing plan for steel producers.   use Facebook and email as the channels

**GPT:** Marketing Plan for Steel Producers:

Target Audience: 

1. Construction companies
2. Manufacturing companies
3. Energy and utility companies
4. Automotive companies

Marketing Channels:

1. Facebook Ads: We can create Facebook Ads that target specific demographics, including location, age, job title, interests, and more. We can also use Facebook to retarget people who have visited our website, engaged with our social media posts, or shown interest in our products.

2. Email Marketing: We can use email marketing to reach out to potential clients and share information about our products and services. We can also send out newsletters, product updates, and special promotions to keep our clients engaged and interested.

Facebook Ads Strategy:

1. Ad Content: We can create compelling ad content that showcases our products and services and highlights the benefits of working with us.

2. Targeting: We can target our ads to specific industries and job titles to reach the right people who are most likely to be interested in our products.

3. Retargeting: We can use retargeting to show ads to people who have visited our website but haven't yet made a purchase.

4. Landing Pages: We can create custom landing pages that are tailored to the specific ad campaigns to improve the chances of conversion.

Email Marketing Strategy:

1. Email List: We can build an email list of potential clients and people who have shown interest in our products.

2. Email Content: We can create engaging email content that showcases our products and services and provides value to our subscribers.

3. Segmentation: We can segment our email list to send targeted messages to specific groups of people, such as those in the construction industry or those interested in a specific product.

4. Call-to-Action: We can include clear calls-to-action in our emails to encourage people to visit our website, make a purchase, or contact us for more information.

Points of Contact:

1. Phone: 555-555-5555
2. Email: sales@steelproducers.com
3. Website: www.steelproducers.com

Email Marketing Example:

Subject: Discover the Benefits of Working with Steel Producers

Dear [First Name],

Are you looking for a reliable and experienced steel producer for your construction, manufacturing, or automotive needs? Look no further than Steel Producers.

At Steel Producers, we specialize in providing high-quality steel products and services to businesses across the country. With years of experience in the industry, we have the expertise and knowledge to meet your unique needs and exceed your expectations.

Our products include everything from steel beams and bars to customized parts and components. We also offer a wide range of services, including design, fabrication, and installation. Whatever your steel needs may be, we have the solutions to help.

ABOUT US
When a great used car inventory meets exceptional customer care, you get the America Auto Wholesale Inc experience.

From cup holders to towing capacity, we are confident that we can match you up with the right used vehicle for your needs. We have a great selection of today's top auto brands and a variety of low-mileage, high-value cars, pickups, and SUVs for almost every budget. We invite you to stop by and browse our inventory of previously loved, well-maintained used vehicles.

The America Auto Wholesale Inc sales department is dedicated to the needs of our customers first. We listen to our customers to understand your automotive needs and budget. We offer vehicle history reports for every vehicle on our lot and our sales staff will work for you to make sure you get the financing you deserve.

If we don't have the vehicle on our lot that suits you and your family, we'll tell you which lot does. Why do we do this? Because we want to earn your trust every time you walk on our lot. America Auto Wholesale Inc does all of this because our customers come first. Always.

**Mike:** how can a car dealer use Facebook market place to advertise ?

**GPT:** Car dealers can use Facebook Marketplace to advertise by creating a listing for their vehicles for sale. To do this, they would need to follow these steps:

1. Go to Facebook Marketplace and click on "Create New Listing"
2. Choose the category "Vehicles"
3. Enter the details of the vehicle, including make, model, year, mileage, price, and photos
4. Set the location of the listing
5. Publish the listing

Facebook Marketplace allows users to filter their search results by location, price range, make and model, and other criteria, so it can be an effective way for car dealers to reach potential buyers in their local area.

Car dealers can also use Facebook Ads to reach a broader audience with their vehicle listings. They can create targeted ads that will be shown to Facebook users who are likely to be interested in buying a car based on their demographics, interests, and online behavior.

To create a Facebook ad, a car dealer would need to follow these steps:

1. Go to Facebook Ads Manager and click on "Create Ad"
2. Choose the objective of the ad (e.g., "Traffic" to drive more people to their website)
3. Select the audience they want to target (e.g., people in their local area who have shown an interest in cars)
4. Choose the ad placement (e.g., Facebook News Feed, Facebook Marketplace, Instagram)
5. Set the budget and schedule for the ad
6. Create the ad creative (e.g., a photo or video of the vehicle, a description of its features and benefits)
7. Publish the ad

By using both Facebook Marketplace and Facebook Ads, car dealers can effectively reach potential buyers on Facebook and increase their chances of making a sale.

**Mike:** is this an effective strategy to use for auto dealers , Facebook market place ?

**GPT:** Yes, using Facebook Marketplace can be an effective strategy for auto dealers. Facebook Marketplace has a large audience, with millions of users browsing the platform every day, and it allows dealerships to reach potential customers who may not have visited their website or physical location. It also offers a convenient and user-friendly platform for customers to browse and purchase vehicles.

In addition, Facebook Marketplace offers various targeting options, allowing dealerships to tailor their ads to specific demographics and interests, increasing the chances of reaching the right audience. Furthermore, the platform offers the ability to track ad performance and make adjustments in real-time to optimize results.

Overall, incorporating Facebook Marketplace into an auto dealership's marketing strategy can be an effective way to increase visibility, generate leads, and ultimately drive sales.

How Casual Style Became the Foundation of Fashion
Workplace shifts, generational differences and the quest for comfort have all played a role. Plus, take a look at five other hot apparel trends for 2023.

Back in 2005, members of Northwestern University women’s lacrosse team wore flip-flops (albeit fancy rhinestone-bedecked ones) to meet the president for a White House ceremony honoring their NCAA championship. The resulting “flip-flop flap,” as many dubbed it, ruled the news for several days – with the nation debating whether it was a breach of etiquette or just a signal of changing generational tastes.

Fast forward to 2017: Two teens were barred from boarding a United Airlines flight because they were wearing leggings, which went against the airline’s dress code for travelers using an employee pass. The decision set off a firestorm on social media with many decrying what they saw as, at best, an out-of-touch company policy and, at worst, policing of women’s clothing choices.

Then came the pandemic, with its suspension of sartorial norms. Even Anna Wintour – the Vogue editor who once claimed never to wear sweatpants – posted an Instagram pic where she sported red trackpants in her home office. Clearly, the fashion police were off duty during quarantine.

But once the world reopened, it became obvious that COVID had just accelerated an already snowballing trend. Would the flip-flop flap have dominated discourse today the same way it did two decades ago? Has the question of whether leggings are pants been resolved once and for all? Barring a few curmudgeonly holdouts, the answers are no and yes, respectively. Everything is casual now – and that’s not likely to change anytime soon.

“It’s definitely comfort, hands down,” says Julie Crawley, director of product development for Top 40 company. “We became acquainted with our sweatpants and loungewear during COVID, and we don’t want to give them up. We still love style and want to look good in the latest trends, but we consistently look for comfort; any fabric that’s super soft is very appealing.”

And many offices have relaxed their dress codes as a welcoming gesture toward staff, says Catherine Tremblay, director of merchandising for apparel private brands. “Employees continue to see evolving workspace landscapes from different forms of hybrid structures, to varied remote settings,” she says. “Even if a workplace has gone back to completely in-person staffing, it seems that the days of having to ‘dress up’ for the office are all but extinct.”

‘TAILOR-MADE’ FOR THE OFFICE
Athleisure has been the norm outside of the office for years, but it’s only in the aftermath of the pandemic that it’s become completely accepted at work. “Where top management has generally not wanted to compromise on dress codes, that’s now shifted,” says Marcus Davis, product development manager for HanesBrands. “Employers and employees are working to find a middle ground.”

The North End sweater
The North End sweater fleece quarter-zip shows how fleece can have a more tailored look.

That could mean trading in joggers for cinched-leg sweats; swapping out hoodies for quarter-zips; or donning a polo instead of a T-shirt, Davis adds. For women, leggings or double-knit stretch pants might be paired with a cardigan for a dressier look. “A button-front loose kind of cardigan concept for a woman is every bit as dressed up as you need to be,” says Vicki Ostrom, a futurist and trend analyst with Top 40 company. “That’s your blazer now.”

Even on notoriously buttoned-up Wall Street, the new work uniform consists of a nice shirt and pants (preferably with some degree of stretch to them), and ties come out only if client meetings are on the agenda, according to a recent New York Times piece on how “business bros” are dressing in their “native habitat.”

Comfort Wash garment-dyed quarter-zip
Ideal for “workleisure,” this new Comfort Wash garment-dyed quarter-zip is polished enough for the office and relaxed enough for everything else.

Comfort, Crawley notes, doesn’t have to translate to sloppy and disheveled. Instead, she adds, it’s all about the use of “soft, tactile, lightweight fabrics with Lycra that help promote range of movement.”

The term “tailored” itself has taken on a different meaning in this era of dressing. Whereas before it may have conjured up the image of three-piece suits and custom-fitted Oxford button-down shirts (items that were actually altered by a tailor), these days a tailored piece tends to refer simply to embellishments that give a garment a more dressed-up look. “There are these signifiers that bring you to the idea that something is tailored,” Ostrom says. “I’ve noticed this a lot with pants. It could be a jogger made of sweatshirt fleece, but it will have this stitched-down pin tuck all the way down the front, so it makes it look like it’s been pressed into a crease.”

GapFit hoodie
This GapFit hoodie shows how small touches can give fleece an elevated, tailored look.

Metal tipping on drawcords, taping around the zipper and other modern touches can give even a hoodie that tailored touch, says Lauren Cocco, director of merchandising at apparel company. “I would say,” she adds, ‘tailored’ is referring to some sort of elevated detail in the design of the garment.”

REAL WORKWEAR & HUNDRED-DOLLAR HOODIES
One factor in the casualization of fashion is the sustained popularity of workwear – think Carhartt jackets, Dickies pants and old-school Levi’s denim – among the younger generations. They like the rugged aesthetic of these blue-collar brands, and the fact that garments are made of long-lasting fabrics doesn’t hurt.

There’s even an element of sustainability to workwear, Ostrom says. “Just by the fact that it’s such a durable fabric, it will last a long time and that means a lot to young people that you keep it in circularity,” she adds. “I think a lot of them are leaning heavily into workwear for that reason.”

Mercer+Mettle open front cardigan
This Mercer+Mettle open front cardigan is a great example of the new office uniform – relaxed, while still looking polished.

Cargo pants and jackets – with their deep, roomy pockets – are also trending. “Utility style,” as Cocco calls it, is hot precisely because of how useful and practical it is for the wearer.

Capitalizing on the trend, Carhartt and other workwear brands have developed more fashion-forward lines, Ostrom says. “It’s their regular workwear, but has a different fit and isn’t quite as oversized,” she adds. “That’s something you could wear more easily in an office situation.”

Another element at play is the economy. With inflation cutting into budgets, many people don’t want – and can’t afford – to maintain multiple wardrobes. So, their apparel purchases have to do double and even triple duty.

Camber hoodie
Camber makes sought-after hoodies and other basics, with consumers often signing on for a months-long wait after ordering one of its garments.

“I think people are choosing versatility,” Crawley says. “If I buy a garment, I want it to serve a few different purposes. Can I wear it to work? To the gym? To the grocery store? Does it pack well for traveling? And can I wear it year-round?”

With that mindset, a high-end hoodie or well-made, durable chore jacket can actually become an investment piece. Cary Heller, vice president of sales apparel company, has seen this phenomenon first-hand, thanks to All USA’s partnership with Camber, a Norristown, PA-based clothing manufacturer for making “the World’s Greatest Sweatshirt.”

Camber’s small stateside team of 25 cuts and sews sweatshirts, hoodies and other basics built to last. “When they shrink up a little, it feels like you’re wearing a tank, almost like a second or third layer of skin,” Heller says. “The only way to fully understand the depth and breadth of how different it is from other hoodies is to put one on. If you buy a product from Camber, even if you’re digging ditches or working as a lineman, you’re going to be wearing it for 50 years. They only get better with washes, not worse.”

That quality comes with a cost: nearly $100 and a months-long waiting list. As GQ writer Noah Johnson described it, “The company makes sweatshirts like Rolex makes watches, which is to say with a degree of care that borders on the mythic.”

The brand is popular in the tech industry, Heller notes. “Those kinds of guys are investing more in an outfit. They’re looking to pay special attention to find something different and something unique, since they know they’ll be wearing it every day,” he adds. “It’s not fast fashion. But this type of person is willing to search out that product and wait for it.”

Younger shoppers are also thinking of the booming secondhand-clothing market when they make a purchase. So, many are willing to drop hundreds of dollars on a designer hoodie, for example, because “they’re expecting that’s an investment they’re also then going to resell,” Ostrom says.

Buying well-made garments – even if they’d traditionally be considered casual – also helps elevate your wardrobe.

“By choosing items that have more quality construction and fabrications,” Tremblay says, “consumers can not only increase the shelf-life of their wardrobe, but it will give a more polished overall look, when paired with dressier items and accessories.”

5 HOT APPAREL TRENDS FOR 2023
1. PATCHWORK & DIY STYLING
Doodlage
Doodlage, a zero-waste brand out of India, upcycles post-consumer waste and post-cutting scraps into new garments, such as this quilted patchwork trench coat.

Handmade items and crafty looks are all the rage on TikTok. “Whether it’s deconstructing blanks and tapestries and making them into jackets/hoodies or creating patchwork and turning it into shirts and pants, having a one-of-a-kind garment is the style,” says Jeremy Picker, creative director and CEO of apparel design and branded merchandise firm AMB3R Creative.

2. PREPPY & COLLEGIATE LOOKS
Mercer+Mettle stretch jersey
Polo shirts, like this Mercer+Mettle stretch jersey piece, are classic and aspirational in uncertain times.

This style conjures up images of Ivy Leaguers with popped collars and perennial tans – an attractive prospect in these unsettled days. “It’s essentially just a simple, useful, functional look that just feels right,” says Vicki Ostrom. “We’re potentially heading into a recession and costs are high, but a polo is always accessible. Everyone can buy into that look.” The other factor, she says, is the growth of “court sports” like tennis and pickleball. Wearing a polo with a crisp tennis skirt helps you “be part of a club,” Ostrom says.

3. SUBTLE CROP TOPS
TriDri cropped hoodie
Cropped tops, where the hem of the shirt meets the top of the pant, are on trend – as evidenced by this TriDri cropped hoodie.

Cropped tops with a “meet-and-greet length – like 22 inches – where the bottom of the shirt just edges the top of the pant” will be big this year, as will cropped hoodies and fleece, according to Marcus Davis. A recent New York Times feature noted that young people have been wearing crop tops in the office, pairing them with high-waisted skirts or pants and adding a cardigan or blazer for extra coverage.

4. SUSTAINABILITY
stretch polo
This stretch polo is made of 44% REPREVE recycled polyester as well as regular polyester and spandex.

Eco-friendly materials and responsibly made garments will continue to grow throughout 2023. And more apparel will highlight the sustainability of their product lines. Julie Crawley of Charles River Apparel says the supplier is partnering with Nexgen Packaging to develop sustainable packaging and recycled hangtags. It’s also working with Unifi to add REPREVE, a brand of recycled polyester, to its collection.

5. RICH EARTH TONES
Mercer+Mettle stretch jersey
Earthy greens, like this quarter-zip from Champion, are the new neutral.

Though playful and uplifting pinks and reds will be popular this year, many color palettes will stick to browns, tans and other outdoorsy “shades from nature,” says Lauren Cocco of Vantage Apparel. “We’re seeing a continued trend toward earth tones and a spectrum of neutrals becoming more sought after.”

READ MORE
Sophisticated Business and Executive Bags
Today’s road warriors require a bag for hauling important information to and from the office and on and off planes. But their preferences are as unique and varying as the industries that employ them.

According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), the U.S. economy added 223,000 jobs in December – more than analysts had expected. Meanwhile, unemployment fell to 3.5%, a historically low rate, BLS reports.

Travis & Wells Transit Computer Portfolio

The Travis & Wells Transit Computer Portfolio has a multifunction organizer with RFID card pockets, mesh smartphone pocket, keychain clip and anti-slip silicone work surface. Also features individual compartments for a tablet and laptop, as well as an adjustable, removable padded shoulder strap.

engraved laptop brief bag

Function and fashion combine in this engraved laptop brief bag. Features include an adjustable shoulder strap, two zip pockets, laptop pocket and multiple accessory pockets.

Oakley Enduro 20L backpack

High-end backpacks are a great gift. This Oakley Enduro 20L backpack features a padded laptop sleeve, fleece-lined eyewear/media pocket, interior organizer panel and mesh water bottle pockets.

genuine-leather computer portfolio

This genuine-leather computer portfolio has five large, zip compartments and a front half-moon zip-pocket with full organizer. In addition, there’s a small organizer, a padded laptop pocket and three file dividers. Choose from saddle, chocolate or black.

Sound Wave hybrid brief/backpack

Users can pick between two functions with this affordable Sound Wave hybrid brief/backpack. Includes a zip main compartment with padded laptop sleeve and full-width zip pocket with a built-in slot for earphones.

Duluth Pack executive briefcase

The Duluth Pack executive briefcase combines 15-ounce canvas with a full-grain leather flap and bottom. Features two interior full-length pockets with ample storage, two large exterior pockets on the front, and a zip pocket on the back. Hand-stitched and made in the USA.

Briggs & Riley Delve slim laptop sleeve

Laptop protection is job #1 for the Briggs & Riley Delve slim laptop sleeve. It’s lightweight and made with RapidWipe-coated outer fabric that protects devices from questionable surfaces in airports, hotels and coffee shops.

**GPT:** The article discusses how the COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated the trend towards casual fashion. Employees who now work remotely or in hybrid structures no longer need to adhere to strict dress codes, and this change is expected to continue even after the pandemic. Athleisure, which has been popular outside of the office for years, is now becoming accepted at work. This shift towards casual attire is also due to changes in generational preferences, as younger consumers lean heavily into workwear for their durability and sustainability. The article also lists five hot apparel trends for 2023, which include patchwork and DIY styling, preppy and collegiate looks, subtle crop tops, sustainability, and rich earth tones.
